# Shannon Brazier Obituary

Obituary published on Legacy.com by Woodbine Funeral Home - Hickory Chapel on Jun. 4, 2026.

It is with heavy hearts that we share that on June 2, 2026, at approximately 1:15 a.m., Shannon Jean Brazier entered into the arms of her Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ, surrounded by family and friends.Shannon was a loving wife, mother, "Lovey," and friend to countless people. She left a remarkable impact on everyone she encountered through her larger-than-life personality, infectious sense of humor, and unmatched compassion. Her ability to make others feel loved, welcomed, and valued was a gift that touched the lives of all who knew her.Though her family mourns her earthly absence, they find comfort in knowing she has been made whole and is now resting in the presence of her Savior. Her legacy of faith, strength, laughter, and unconditional love will continue to live on in the hearts of those blessed enough to have known her.Shannon was born on September 16, 1971, in Columbia, Tennessee, to Gary Leon King and Sharmin Jean Rowlett. She attended McGavock High School and later pursued higher education at Middle Tennessee State University.Professionally, Shannon served as a Classroom Associate at Granbery Elementary School, where she found tremendous joy in working with children. She deeply loved her students and cherished the opportunity to encourage, support, and make a difference in their lives each day.While Shannon held many roles throughout her life, none were more important to her than being a mother and grandmother. Her children were the center of her world, and becoming "Lovey" brought her immeasurable joy. Known affectionately as both "Lovey" and "GiGi," she treasured every moment spent with her grandchildren and those she loved as her own. She never missed an opportunity to spoil them, encourage them, and create lasting memories together.A woman of tremendous generosity and faith, Shannon faithfully supported St. Jude Children's Research Hospital and frequently volunteered her time to help others. Her compassionate spirit and servant's heart were evident in every aspect of her life.Shannon loved shopping, family vacations to the beach, and finding reasons to gather the people she loved together. She believed family was life's greatest blessing and worked tirelessly to keep those connections strong. Whether planning a vacation, organizing a family gathering, or simply checking in on someone she loved, Shannon's presence brought people together.Those who knew Shannon could always count on her famous green bean casserole to appear at family meals-often arriving a little later than planned but always worth the wait. Her desserts quickly became family favorites, and no gathering felt complete without one of her sweet creations.Shannon was known for her honesty, her quick wit, and her unforgettable sense of humor. She had a unique way of saying exactly what was on her mind, whether people were ready to hear it or not. Her authenticity, fierce loyalty, and ability to bring laughter into even the most difficult moments made her truly one of a kind.Shannon was preceded in death by her mother, Sharmin Jean Rowlett; her beloved nephew, Dylan King; her cousin, Jennifer Baker; her paternal grandparents, Eva and Earnest King; and her maternal grandparents, Zettie and Walter Dugger.She is survived by her devoted husband, Terry Brazier; her children, Chelsea Taylor Glover, Macey Hannah Reese, and Collin Parker Brazier; her father, Gary Leon King; her bonus mother, Vickie King; her brother, Gary Kevin King and his life partner, Kate; her sister, Brittany Michelle Legg and her husband, Andrew Legg; her grandchildren, Drew Glover, Makyna Glover, Palmer Glover, Piper Glover, Beau Reese, and Belle Reese; Logan Kropog and Millie Chapman, whom she lovingly considered her grandchildren; her cousin, Amberly Chapman; and Chelsey Rogers, whom she considered a bonus daughter; along with countless nieces, nephews, cousins, extended family members, friends, coworkers, students, and all whose lives were blessed by knowing her.The family will receive friends on Friday, June 5, 2026, from 10:00 a.m. until 2:30 p.m. at Woodbine Funeral Home, Hickory Chapel. A funeral service will follow at 3:00 p.m., with interment immediately afterward at Nolensville Cemetery.Shannon's family will forever cherish the love, laughter, guidance, and unwavering devotion she shared throughout her life. Though her absence leaves an immeasurable void, they find peace in knowing that she is rejoicing in Heaven and hearing the words, "Well done, good and faithful servant."

To plant trees in memory, please visit the Sympathy Store.

2335 St. Joseph Avenue
Saint Joseph, Missouri
# Shannon Church Obituary
Shannon Leigh Church, age 39, of St. Joseph, Mo., passed away on Friday, Dec. 18, 2020.
Shannon was born in Quincy, Ill., to mother, Mary L. Church, and father, Ronald Altheide. She spent her formative years in Quincy and attended Quincy schools. In her mid-twenties, she relocated to St. Joseph.
Shannon enjoyed spending time with family and friends, watching true-crime dramas and nature shows and doing crafts with her youngest child.
Shannon was preceeded in death by her father and her maternal grandfather, Orion H. Church.
Survivors include her five children; Makenzie, Craig, Joseph, Khloe and Maleigha (Coburn); her mother, her brother, Dana W. Church and her maternal grandmother, Donna M. Church. Also surviving are her aunts, Michelle (Church) Smyser; Gail Church and uncles, Charles H. and John W. Church, plus numerous cousins.
Shannon was cremated under the direction of Simplify Cremation and Funerals. Inurnment will be at Curless Cemetery at a later date.
A Celebration of Shannon's Life will be held on Saturday, June 26, 2021, at the Eagles Club on North 5th Street, beginning at 3 p.m.
Family and friends are invited to come and share fond memories and happy or funny anecdotes of time spent with her.
After the sharing, family and close friends are invited to remain for refreshment, food and music. No alcohol will be provided; it will be BYOB.

# Shannon Warren Obituary

Obituary published on Legacy.com by Browning Funeral Home - Evansville on Jun. 8, 2026.

Shannon Winfield Warren, 48, of Chandler, Indiana, passed away Sunday, June 7, 2026, at home.Shannon was born in Evansville, Indiana, on November 9, 1977. He worked as a supervisor at Century Aluminum. Shannon loved his family and was very dedicated to his children and grandchildren. In his free time, he enjoyed shooting guns and spending time with those he loved.Shannon is survived by his wife, Melissa (Weber) Warren of Chandler, IN. The couple shared 32 years together and were married in 2009. He is also survived by his children, Keenan Warren (Maicey Washington) of Chandler, IN, Jayden Wahl of Evansville, IN and Keeana Warren of Chandler, IN; mother, Jacqueline Warren of Evansville, IN; twin brother, Leshaun Warren (Noni) of Texas; grandchildren, Kingstyn, Kaizen and Kaliyah Warren, Nyla and Jayden Jr. Wahl, and Ke'Ariah; father-in-law, Steve Weber of Chandler, IN; and best friend, Roy Burkins (Amanda) of Evansville, IN.Shannon was preceded in death by his grandparents, Elsie and Lawrence Warren; mother-in-law, Janelle Weber; and grandchild, Kase Warren.A gathering for family and friends to celebrate Shannon's life will be held from 12:00 noon until 6:00 pm on Friday, June 12, 2026, at Browning Funeral Home, 738 E. Diamond Ave., Evansville, IN 47711.

To plant trees in memory, please visit the Sympathy Store.

# Shannon Warren Obituary
...
Obituary published on Legacy.com by Browning Funeral Home - Evansville on Jun. 8, 2026.
...
Shannon Winfield Warren, 48, of Chandler, Indiana, passed away Sunday, June 7, 2026, at home.Shannon was born in Evansville, Indiana, on November 9, 1977. He worked as a supervisor at Century Aluminum. Shannon loved his family and was very dedicated to his children and grandchildren. In his free time, he enjoyed shooting guns and spending time with those he loved.Shannon is survived by his wife, Melissa (Weber) Warren of Chandler, IN. The couple shared 32 years together and were married in 2009. He is also survived by his children, Keenan Warren (Maicey Washington) of Chandler, IN, Jayden Wahl of Evansville, IN and Keeana Warren of Chandler, IN; mother, Jacqueline Warren of Evansville, IN; twin brother, Leshaun Warren (Noni) of Texas; grandchildren, Kingstyn, Kaizen and Kaliyah Warren, Nyla and Jayden Jr. Wahl, and Ke'Ariah; father-in-law, Steve Weber of Chandler, IN; and best friend, Roy Burkins (Amanda) of Evansville, IN.Shannon was preceded in death by his grandparents, Elsie and Lawrence Warren; mother-in-law, Janelle Weber; and grandchild, Kase Warren.A gathering for family and friends to celebrate Shannon's life will be held from 12:00 noon until 6:00 pm on Friday, June 12, 2026, at Browning Funeral Home, 738 E. Diamond Ave., Evansville, IN 47711.
